phonics.description: The letter A makes the 'ah' sound like in 'apple'.
phonics.rule: When A is by itself or before a consonant in a short word, it usually makes the short 'ah' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Which word starts with the letter A? (cat, ant, dog)

phonics.description: The letter B makes the 'buh' sound like in 'ball'. Your lips press together and then pop open!
phonics.rule: B always makes the same 'buh' sound. Press your lips together to make it!
phonics.practice_question: Can you think of another word that starts with the letter B like 'ball'?

phonics.description: The letter C usually makes the 'kuh' sound like in 'cat'. It sounds like a cat saying 'k'!
phonics.rule: C makes the 'kuh' sound when followed by A, O, or U. It makes the 'sss' sound when followed by E or I.
phonics.practice_question: Does the word 'cat' start with a C sound or an S sound?

phonics.description: The letter D makes the 'duh' sound like in 'dog'. Your tongue taps the top of your mouth!
phonics.rule: D always makes the same 'duh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: What sound does D make? Say 'dog' and listen for it!

phonics.description: The letter E makes the 'eh' sound like in 'egg'.
phonics.rule: When E is by itself in a short word, it usually makes the short 'eh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: What starts with the E sound: egg or apple?

phonics.description: The letter F makes the 'fff' sound like in 'fish'. Your top teeth touch your bottom lip!
phonics.rule: F always makes the same 'fff' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'fish' - what letter does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter G usually makes the 'guh' sound like in 'goat'. It comes from the back of your throat!
phonics.rule: G makes the 'guh' sound when followed by A, O, or U.
phonics.practice_question: Does 'goat' start with a G? Say it and listen!

phonics.description: The letter H makes the 'huh' sound like in 'hat'. It's like a little puff of air!
phonics.rule: H always makes the same breathy 'huh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Which word starts with H: hat or cat?

phonics.description: The letter I makes the 'ih' sound like in 'igloo'.
phonics.rule: When I is by itself in a short word, it usually makes the short 'ih' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'igloo' - what is the first sound you hear?

phonics.description: The letter J makes the 'juh' sound like in 'jump'. Your tongue comes near the top of your mouth!
phonics.rule: J always makes the same 'juh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Can you jump? What letter does 'jump' start with?

phonics.description: The letter K makes the 'kuh' sound like in 'kite'. It sounds just like C in 'cat'!
phonics.rule: K always makes the 'kuh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Which word starts with K: kite or bite?

phonics.description: The letter L makes the 'lll' sound like in 'lion'. Your tongue touches the top of your mouth!
phonics.rule: L always makes the same smooth 'lll' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'lion' slowly. What is the first sound?

phonics.description: The letter M makes the 'mmm' sound like in 'moon'. Your lips press together!
phonics.rule: M always makes the same 'mmm' sound. You can hum it!
phonics.practice_question: Hum 'mmm' - that's the M sound! What does 'moon' start with?

phonics.description: The letter N makes the 'nnn' sound like in 'nest'. Your tongue touches the top of your mouth!
phonics.rule: N always makes the same 'nnn' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'nest' slowly. What is the first sound?

phonics.description: The letter O makes the 'oh' sound like in 'octopus'.
phonics.rule: When O is by itself in a short word, it usually makes the short 'oh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'octopus' - what sound does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter P makes the 'puh' sound like in 'pig'. Your lips pop open like a balloon popping!
phonics.rule: P always makes the same 'puh' popping sound.
phonics.practice_question: Put your hand near your mouth and say 'pig' - do you feel a little puff of air?

phonics.description: The letter Q almost always goes with U! Together QU makes the 'kwuh' sound like in 'queen'.
phonics.rule: Q is almost always followed by U. Together they make the 'kwuh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: What sound do Q and U make together? Like in 'queen'!

phonics.description: The letter R makes the 'rrr' sound like in 'rabbit'. Your tongue curls a little bit!
phonics.rule: R always makes the same 'rrr' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'rabbit' - what sound does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter S makes the 'sss' sound like in 'sun'. It sounds like a snake!
phonics.rule: S usually makes the 'sss' sound like a snake hissing.
phonics.practice_question: Make the S sound! What does 'sun' start with?

phonics.description: The letter T makes the 'tuh' sound like in 'tiger'. Your tongue taps the top of your mouth!
phonics.rule: T always makes the same 'tuh' tapping sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'tiger' - what sound does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter U makes the 'uh' sound like in 'umbrella'.
phonics.rule: When U is by itself in a short word, it usually makes the short 'uh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'umbrella' - what sound does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter V makes the 'vvv' sound like in 'van'. Your top teeth touch your bottom lip!
phonics.rule: V always makes the same buzzing 'vvv' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'van' - feel your teeth touch your lip for the V sound!

phonics.description: The letter W makes the 'wuh' sound like in 'whale'. Your lips make a round shape!
phonics.rule: W always makes the same 'wuh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Round your lips and say 'whale' - that's the W sound!

phonics.description: The letter X usually makes the 'ks' sound like at the end of 'fox'. It's two sounds together!
phonics.rule: X at the end of words makes the 'ks' sound. At the start, it can sound like 'z'.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'fox' - do you hear the X sound at the end?

phonics.description: The letter Y makes the 'yuh' sound like in 'yellow'. Your mouth smiles a little!
phonics.rule: Y at the start of words makes the 'yuh' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Say 'yellow' - what sound does it start with?

phonics.description: The letter Z makes the 'zzz' sound like in 'zebra'. It buzzes like a bee!
phonics.rule: Z always makes the buzzing 'zzz' sound.
phonics.practice_question: Buzz like a bee - that's the Z sound! What does 'zebra' start with?

sight_words.sentence: The cat sat on the mat.
sight_words.practice_question: Can you find the word 'the' in this sentence: 'The dog runs fast'?

sight_words.sentence: I have a cat and a dog.
sight_words.practice_question: Use the word 'and' to join two things you like.
Fill in the blank: The apple ___ red.

sight_words.sentence: The fish lives in the water.
sight_words.practice_question: Where does the fish live? Find the word 'in'!
Can you say a sentence using the word 'it'?

blends.description: SH together make the 'shh' sound like when you say 'be quiet!' to someone.
blends.practice_question: What sound do S and H make together? Like in 'ship'!

blends.description: CH together make the 'chuh' sound like in 'chair'. Like a train going 'choo choo'!
blends.practice_question: Say 'chair' - what two letters make the CH sound?

blends.description: TH together make the 'thh' sound like in 'think'. Put your tongue between your teeth!
blends.practice_question: Stick your tongue out a tiny bit and say 'think' - that's the TH sound!

blends.description: BL together make the 'bl' sound like in 'blue'. Two sounds blended together!
blends.practice_question: Say 'blue' - can you hear both the B and L sounds blended together?
